Vinola Lopez, Postdoctoral Research Scientist, and Ken Marks, Fossil Preparator, as they showcase recently unearthed fossils from a 7-million-year-old site in the Caribbean. events.title: Papua New Guinea Collections Celebration
events.location_address: 1400 S Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60605
events.description: Come meet Field Museum Collections staff Chris Philipp and Julia Kennedy as they discuss rarely-displayed masks from the A.B. Lewis collection, new contemporary items, and various media from the Field's Papua New Guinea collection. At nearly 35,000 items, the Field Museum cares for the largest collection of material from the country of Papua New Guinea currently in the United States. This session is the fourth and final in our series that celebrates Wan Bel: aCollective Global Exhibition , commemorating the country of Papua New Guinea's 50th Anniversary of Independence.

events.title: Bee Curious: Up Close & Un-bee-lievable
events.location_address: 1400 S Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60605
events.description: Join us in celebration of World Bee Day with scientists from the Negaunee Integrative Research and Keller Science Action Centers! This engaging walk-in session dives into the fascinating world of global and local pollinators, focusing on the un-bee-lievable ones and the role they play in keeping our ecosystems blooming. During this interactive presentation, you’ll get a bee-hind-the-scenes look at real bee specimens from around the world, learn how to identify common native species, and uncover the sweet details of their life cycles, habitats, and behaviors. We hope that guests will learn about how people, plants, and pollinators are all connected and that they can help pollinators too. Presenters include Bruno de Medeiros, Assistant Curator of Insects; Aster Hasle, Lead Conservation Ecologist and Nicole Machuca, Environmental Social Scientist.
